_comment: Content ladder for the autonomous content runs. Each run builds the first entry whose status is 'todo', wires it to be reachable in play, passes the self-verify gate (scripts/selfplay.mjs + size-limit), then flips it to 'done'. Never repeat a done entry.

ladder.summary: Populate lj.enemy.types.blue {a,b,c,d,B} (frost/arcane theme) and add lj.enemy.familyForRealm(size) so realm 2+ spawns the blue family. Reuses existing a/b/c/d/B tile symbols (single-row sprite sheet has no per-color art).

Populate lj.enemy.types.green {a,b,c,d,B} ("The Blightwood" poison/nature theme) and extend the familyForRealm ladder so realm 3+ spawns green. familyLadder auto-picks up the populated family; realm 3=green, realm 4+ clamp to green. Step over blue kept tiny (<=1.17x agility/HP, armor untouched) because the realm-3+ clamp compounds hard with 1.4^(size-1) scaling.

Populate lj.enemy.types.red {a,b,c,d,B} ("The Emberdeep" molten/infernal theme) so the familyLadder auto-routes realm 4+ to red. Reuses a/b/c/d/B tile symbols (already in spriteMap + creaturesAndItemsMap). Red now owns realm 4+ — which selfplay shows is essentially the whole decisive game (nearly every win/death) — so green retreats to realm 3 alone and the green->red step is deliberately gentle: armor UNTOUCHED on every grade (it is a % dmg-reduction that compounds hardest with depth), escalation is +1 HP/grade plus +1 agility on striker+boss and +1 boss crit. A full notch (flat +1 str on striker+boss) cratered the sim to ~24% (below the 25% floor); this holds ~28-29%, a clean ~4pt step below green mirroring blue->green.

Added The Cinderwyrm (red grade "T"), the first boss with a real combat mechanic: ENRAGE. lj.enemy.monster carries an optional mechanic descriptor; lj.enemy.base copies it onto instances; lj.enemy.enraged(enemy,curHp,maxHp) returns a boosted attack snapshot below threshold*maxHp and the fight loop (lj.hero.fight in combat.js) resolves the enemy's swing through it, so damage ramps mid-fight. Reachable: lj.enemy.bossGradeForRealm(size) -> "T" at realm 5+ (else "B"), placed in realm.js, routed in hero.js (creaturesAndItemsMap + lj.enemy.isBoss level-up), rendered via scene.js spriteMap (reuses boss sprite [0,9]); selfplay fights the per-realm boss grade. Tuned as a risk/reward trade: base offense [5,7] gentler than the Emberlord's [7,8], enrage (0.4 threshold, 1.5x str&agi) makes the sub-40% back half hit ~[8,11] — harder than the old boss. Net ~29.3% win rate (A/B: enrage off 33.5% vs on 28.4%, ~5pt swing => load-bearing).
Add a coherent batch of item prefixes/suffixes to js/stats.js and/or enemy mods to js/enemy.js.
Add a new room template / biome variation to js/realm.js gen, with matching spriteMap coverage.
Data-driven rebalance pass guided by scripts/selfplay.mjs output; only if the win rate has drifted toward a band edge.
